outcomes: Apply calorimetry techniques to measure enthalpy changes (∆U and ∆H)
Distinguish between different types of reaction enthalpies
Predict the spontaneity of reactions using Gibbs Energy Change
Relate equilibrium constants to reaction quotients and Gibbs energy
Explain dynamic equilibrium in physical and chemical processes
Apply the Law of Chemical Equilibrium to determine equilibrium constants
Analyze homogeneous and heterogeneous equilibria
Predict shifts in equilibrium based on concentration, temperature, and pressure changes
Calculate pH and buffer capacity in ionic solutions
Classify reactions based on classical and electron transfer definitions of redox
Determine oxidation numbers for elements in compounds
Analyze electrode processes in redox reactions

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Organic Chemistry – Some Basic Principles and Techniques
learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Organic Chemistry – Some Basic Principles and Techniques
outcomes: Represent organic structures using standard conventions
Apply IUPAC nomenclature rules to classify organic compounds
Identify and differentiate between various types of isomers
Analyze fundamental concepts in organic reaction mechanisms
Execute qualitative and quantitative analysis of organic compounds
Classify hydrocarbons into alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and aromatic compounds
Correlate structure with physical properties of hydrocarbons
Evaluate the carcinogenic potential and toxicity of specific hydrocarbons
Relate electronic configuration to position in the periodic table
Analyze trends in atomic/ionic radii, ionization enthalpy, and hydration enthalpy
Identify unique behavior of the first element in each group
Apply qualitative gas laws to predict state changes
Analyze the ideal gas equation and its limitations
